div.head: The Secretary of State to the Chargé in Cuba (Nufer)
div.p: 704. At the first meeting with Cuban Sugar Commission, afternoon of August 28, Mr. Wilson, CCC, stressed the excellent cooperation of the Cuban Sugar Institute, the hope that these negotiations will cover the last global sugar sale between the two Governments, and the basic nature of this Government’s policy in maintaining the present sugar ceiling price.

A United States proposal,The United States proposal (not printed), offering to purchase Cuba’s 1945 and 1946 sugar crops at a basic price of 2.65 cents per pound f.a.s., with an escalator clause to protect Cuba from higher costs of production, was handed to the Cuban delegation on August 29. along the lines of the enclosure to instruction no. 4412 of August 26,Not printed. will be submitted to the Cuban Delegation on August 29.
